examples.title: Simple C Program Structure
examples.description: Demonstrates the basic structure of a C program following procedural paradigm
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates procedural programming paradigm. It uses functions to organize code into reusable modules. The main() function serves as the entry point, calling the greetUser() function which performs a specific task. The program shows preprocessor directives, function declarations, global variables, local variables, and function definitions.

examples.title: OOP Example in Python
examples.description: Demonstrates Object-Oriented Programming concepts: class, objects, encapsulation, and inheritance
examples.code:
# Class Definition - Blueprint for objects
class Animal:
    # Constructor - initializes object
    def __init__(self, name, species):
        self.name = name  # Attribute
        self.species = species
        self.__age = 0  # Private attribute (Encapsulation)
    
    # Method - function inside class
    def display_info(self):
        print(f"Name: {self.name}, Species: {self.species}")
    
    # Setter method for encapsulated data
    def set_age(self, age):
        self.__age = age
    
    def get_age(self):
        return self.__age

# Inheritance - Dog inherits from Animal
class Dog(Animal):
    def __init__(self, name, breed):
        super().__init__(name, "Canine")  # Call parent constructor
        self.breed = breed
    
    # Polymorphism - same method different behavior
    def display_info(self):
        print(f"{self.name} is a {self.breed} dog")

# Creating Objects
animal1 = Animal("Lion", "Feline")
dog1 = Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")

# Using Methods
animal1.display_info()
dog1.display_info()  # Polymorphism in action
examples.explanation: This Python program demonstrates OOP concepts: (1) Class defines blueprint with attributes and methods, (2) Objects (animal1, dog1) are instances of classes, (3) Encapsulation using private __age with getter/setter, (4) Inheritance - Dog class inherits from Animal, (5) Polymorphism - display_info() behaves differently for Dog than Animal.

examples.title: Procedural Approach Example
examples.description: Shows procedural programming paradigm using functions to solve a problem step by step
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates procedural programming paradigm where the program is organized into functions/subprocedures. Each function performs a specific task (calculateArea, calculatePerimeter). The main procedure orchestrates the flow by calling these functions. Data and functions are separate, contrasting with OOP where they are bundled together.

examples.title: Encapsulation Demonstration
examples.description: Shows how encapsulation is implemented in Java using private variables and public methods
examples.explanation: This Java code demonstrates encapsulation: (1) Private data members (accountNumber, balance) cannot be accessed directly from outside the class, (2) Public methods (getters, deposit, withdraw) provide controlled access, (3) Validation in deposit/withdraw prevents invalid data modification. This protects data integrity and implements data hiding - a key OOP principle.
